warnings: Significant ink bleed and shadowing from the reverse side is visible, particularly affecting the clarity of text and numerical values in rows 15-28.
The vertical ruling lines separating the Pounds, Shillings, and Pence columns are extremely faint or nearly invisible, especially in the upper and middle sections of the page.
Light brown stains (foxing) are scattered across the page, lightly obscuring some characters but generally not preventing readability.
layout_hints: The vertical ruling lines for the amount columns (£ s d) are mostly invisible. The final total row (row 29) clearly shows the column separation for '100', '2', and '11 1/2'. Use this row as your primary reference for projecting the column boundaries upwards. Pay close attention to the visual spacing in other rows to maintain alignment.
prompt_additions: CRITICAL: The vertical ruling lines for the money columns (£ s d) are virtually non-existent for most of the page. Use row 29 (the Total row: '100', '2', '11 1/2') to establish precise vertical column boundaries. Project these boundaries upwards for all other rows. Rows 15-28 are moderately obscured by ink bleed from the reverse; if an amount is unreadable, leave it blank and set confidence < 0.3. Row 17 and row 26 are continuations of the description from the previous line but contain their own amounts; treat these as distinct rows. The pence value in the total row (row 29) is '11 1/2'; ensure this fraction is extracted accurately.
